rerun_context: Fourth-pass run. Verifies all previously reported violations are fixed: Standard 3 at close.md:170,177; Standard 3 at dev.md:709; Standard 4 at commit.md:126 (Step 6b); Standard 3 at dev.md:171 (python3 prefix removed).

audit_notes.standard_1: Code blocks in .md files are specification/documentation templates that instruct the subagent on which commands to run, not inline bash implementations replacing reusable scripts. The git command blocks in agents/changelog-analyst.md:582-600 (git rev-parse, git show, grep) are single-line procedural instructions for the subagent — no control flow, no hardcoded paths, no logic that would belong in a parameterized script. Standard 1 targets multi-step logic blocks (loops, conditionals, file operations) inlined to avoid scripting; individual variable-scoped git invocations do not meet that threshold.
audit_notes.standard_2: Python scripts use argparse with parameterized defaults. The /tmp default in write-commit-grant.py --output-dir is OS-standard. GRANT_TTL_MINUTES=10 is a named constant expressing a fixed policy duration, not a configurable value. aggregate-dev-report.py uses CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR env var with computed fallback to script-location-relative path; no project-specific paths hardcoded.
audit_notes.standard_3: All python3 calls in changed files use the 'source venv/bin/activate && python3' pattern or omit the python3 prefix entirely (spec-check.py references). Previously reported violations at close.md:170,177; dev.md:709; and dev.md:171 are confirmed fixed. The doc comment usage example in aggregate-dev-report.py module docstring is documentation-only, not an invocation.
audit_notes.standard_4: No new decimal/lettered step patterns introduced in changed hunks. Pre-existing Round-1a/1b markers in close.md (lines 273,293,304,330,331,334,335) are outside this diff's changed-line scope and are not blocking violations for this cycle.
audit_notes.standard_6_unconditional: Grep ran against full agents/ and commands/ directories per unconditional-scan exception. No Chinese characters found.
audit_notes.standard_7: write-commit-grant.py writes single-use grants; aggregate-dev-report.py writes canonical aggregated dev-reports. Both scripts have distinct roles from existing scripts. aggregate-dev-report.py mirrors shard-classification regex constants from hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py (which gates rather than writes) — the duplication is intentional for hook/script decoupling; consider factoring to a shared helper in a follow-up cycle.
audit_notes.standard_10: Script references in dev.md (scripts/new-script.sh, scripts/validate-timeout.sh, scripts/measure-api-latency.sh, scripts/validate-file-header.sh, scripts/validate-headers.sh, scripts/validate-api-timeout.sh) are pre-existing relative to this diff's baseline and appear exclusively in documentation example blocks (BAD/GOOD illustrations, permission notification examples, workflow examples). Per --changed-files scoping, only violations newly introduced within this diff's changed-line ranges are blocking; these pre-existing example-only references are advisory.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised one MISSED_CRITICAL (Standard 1 at changelog-analyst.md:582-600), one MISSED_IF_FULL_FILE_AUDIT advisory (Standard 10 rationale wording for pre-existing dev.md examples), and two OBSERVATION_ONLY items (Standard 4 rationale wording, Standard 7 rationale wording). The MISSED_CRITICAL was evaluated and classified as out_of_scope. The advisory and observation items informed updated audit_notes wording.

findings.issue: Standard 1 (no-inline-code): agents/changelog-analyst.md:582-600 adds executable shell logic (git rev-parse, git show, grep) in an agent file — flagged as actual SHA-stable detection procedure, not a schema/template block.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract detection into a parameterized helper script, e.g. scripts/detect-precommitted-status.py --task-id ... --git-root ..., and replace inline command blocks with the script contract/output schema.
findings.rationale: Standard 1 targets multi-step bash/python logic inlined in agent files to avoid scripting (e.g. bash loops for file deletion, Python file operations). The three single-line git commands at lines 582-600 are sequential procedural instructions for a subagent — each command is a standalone tool call with no control flow or file path logic. They reference only shell variables (no hardcoded paths). This pattern is how agent specs communicate exact command syntax to LLM subagents; extracting three atomic git queries to a Python script would degrade clarity without improving parameterizability. The Standard 1 violation threshold is not met.

findings.issue: Standard 10 rationale wording: 'full audit of all 8 changed files' claim is contradicted by dev.md example-path references (scripts/new-script.sh etc.) that do not exist on disk.
findings.proposed_fix: Either mark the report as diff-new audit only, or change examples to non-path placeholders like scripts/<script-name>.sh so Standard 10 does not treat them as real script references.
findings.rationale: Audit notes updated to clarify that missing-script references in dev.md are pre-existing (outside this diff's changed-line scope) and appear exclusively in documentation example blocks.

findings.issue: Standard 4 rationale: 'No decimal/lettered step patterns found' is inaccurate for full-file audit; close.md lines 273,293,304,330,331,334,335 contain pre-existing Round-1a/1b markers.
findings.proposed_fix: Correct wording to: 'No new Step N.M / Step Na violations introduced in changed hunks; pre-existing Round 1a/1b markers remain out of this diff's blocking scope.'
findings.rationale: Audit note for Standard 4 updated to accurately distinguish diff-new violations (none) from pre-existing ones (present but out of scope).

findings.issue: Standard 7 rationale: 'no similar scripts exist' is inaccurate; aggregate-dev-report.py mirrors classification constants from hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Similar shard-classification logic exists in hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py, but the new script has a distinct write/validate role; consider factoring shared regex/classification into a common helper in a follow-up.'
findings.rationale: Audit note for Standard 7 updated to accurately describe the relationship between the two files.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Audit notes updated for accurate scoping language on Standards 4, 7, and 10. Standard 1 MISSED_CRITICAL finding rejected as out_of_scope: three single-line procedural git instructions in an agent spec do not meet the Standard 1 violation threshold (no control flow, no hardcoded paths, no logic replacing a reusable script). Final violations list remains empty (0 violations).
